# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.

Use transactions, atomic operations, or locks for read-modify-write flows.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Data Processing Pipeline with Temporary Files and Locking
# Uses predictable temp file names with PID for tracking

set -euo pipefail

# Define predictable temp file paths using PID
TEMP_INPUT="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"
TEMP_PROCESSED="/tmp/myapp-$$-processed.tmp"
TEMP_RESULTS="/tmp/myapp-$$-results.tmp"
LOCK_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$$.lock"
TRANSACTION_LOG="/tmp/myapp-$$-transaction.log"

# Track all temp files for cleanup
TEMP_FILES=("$TEMP_INPUT" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" "$TEMP_RESULTS" "$LOCK_FILE" "$TRANSACTION_LOG")

# --- Cleanup function ---
cleanup() {
    local exit_code=$?
    echo "[CLEANUP] Cleaning up temporary files for PID $$..."
    for f in "${TEMP_FILES[@]}"; do
        if [[ -f "$f" ]]; then
            rm -f "$f"
            echo "[CLEANUP] Removed: $f"
        fi
    done
    # Release lock fd if open
    exec 200>&- 2>/dev/null || true
    echo "[CLEANUP] Done. Exiting with code $exit_code."
    exit "$exit_code"
}

# Register cleanup on EXIT, INT, TERM, HUP
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM HUP

# --- Logging helpers ---
log_transaction() {
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    echo "[$timestamp] $1" >> "$TRANSACTION_LOG"
}

log_info() {
    echo "[INFO] $1"
}

log_error() {
    echo "[ERROR] $1" >&2
}

# --- Locking functions for atomic operations ---
acquire_lock() {
    local max_retries=10
    local retry_delay=1
    local attempt=0

    log_info "Acquiring lock: $LOCK_FILE"

    # Open lock file on fd 200
    exec 200>"$LOCK_FILE"

    while (( attempt < max_retries )); do
        if flock -n 200; then
            log_info "Lock acquired (attempt $((attempt + 1)))"
            log_transaction "LOCK_ACQUIRED"
            return 0
        fi
        attempt=$((attempt + 1))
        log_info "Lock busy, retrying in ${retry_delay}s (attempt $attempt/$max_retries)..."
        sleep "$retry_delay"
    done

    log_error "Failed to acquire lock after $max_retries attempts"
    return 1
}

release_lock() {
    flock -u 200 2>/dev/null || true
    log_info "Lock released"
    log_transaction "LOCK_RELEASED"
}

# --- Atomic write: write to temp then rename (atomic on same filesystem) ---
atomic_write() {
    local target_file="$1"
    local content="$2"
    local staging_file="${target_file}.staging.$$"

    # Write to staging file first
    echo "$content" > "$staging_file"

    # Sync to ensure data is on disk
    sync "$staging_file" 2>/dev/null || true

    # Atomic rename
    mv -f "$staging_file" "$target_file"

    log_transaction "ATOMIC_WRITE: $target_file ($(wc -c < "$target_file") bytes)"
}

# --- Atomic read-modify-write with locking ---
atomic_read_modify_write() {
    local target_file="$1"
    local modify_command="$2"  # A command/function name that reads stdin and writes stdout

    acquire_lock

    local current_content=""
    if [[ -f "$target_file" ]]; then
        current_content=$(cat "$target_file")
    fi

    # Apply modification
    local new_content
    new_content=$(echo "$current_content" | eval "$modify_command")

    # Atomic write
    atomic_write "$target_file" "$new_content"

    log_transaction "READ_MODIFY_WRITE: $target_file via '$modify_command'"

    release_lock
}

# --- Stage 1: Generate input data ---
stage_generate_data() {
    log_info "=== Stage 1: Generating input data ==="
    log_transaction "STAGE_1_BEGIN"

    # Simulate generating data (e.g., from a database, API, or file system)
    local data=""
    data+="timestamp,sensor_id,temperature,humidity,status\n"
    data+="2024-01-15T10:00:00,S001,22.5,45.2,OK\n"
    data+="2024-01-15T10:00:00,S002,25.1,38.7,OK\n"
    data+="2024-01-15T10:00:00,S003,19.8,52.1,WARN\n"
    data+="2024-01-15T10:00:00,S004,31.2,28.4,CRITICAL\n"
    data+="2024-01-15T10:00:00,S005,23.7,41.9,OK\n"
    data+="2024-01-15T10:01:00,S001,22.7,44.8,OK\n"
    data+="2024-01-15T10:01:00,S002,25.3,38.2,OK\n"
    data+="2024-01-15T10:01:00,S003,18.9,55.3,WARN\n"
    data+="2024-01-15T10:01:00,S004,32.1,27.1,CRITICAL\n"
    data+="2024-01-15T10:01:00,S005,23.9,41.5,OK\n"

    # Atomic write to input temp file
    atomic_write "$TEMP_INPUT" "$(echo -e "$data")"

    local line_count
    line_count=$(wc -l < "$TEMP_INPUT")
    log_info "Generated $((line_count - 1)) data records in $TEMP_INPUT"
    log_transaction "STAGE_1_COMPLETE: $((line_count - 1)) records"
}

# --- Stage 2: Process/transform data ---
stage_process_data() {
    log_info "=== Stage 2: Processing data ==="
    log_transaction "STAGE_2_BEGIN"

    if [[ ! -f "$TEMP_INPUT" ]]; then
        log_error "Input file not found: $TEMP_INPUT"
        return 1
    fi

    acquire_lock

    # Read from the same input temp file
    local header
    header=$(head -1 "$TEMP_INPUT")

    # Process: filter, transform, enrich
    # Add a computed column: temp_category (LOW/NORMAL/HIGH/CRITICAL)
    {
        echo "${header},temp_category,alert_level"

        tail -n +2 "$TEMP_INPUT" | while IFS=',' read -r timestamp sensor_id temperature humidity status; do
            # Skip empty lines
            [[ -z "$timestamp" ]] && continue

            # Categorize temperature
            local temp_category="NORMAL"
            local alert_level=0

            # Use awk for floating point comparison
            local temp_int
            temp_int=$(echo "$temperature" | awk '{printf "%d", $1}')

            if (( temp_int < 20 )); then
                temp_category="LOW"
                alert_level=1
            elif (( temp_int >= 20 && temp_int < 25 )); then
                temp_category="NORMAL"
                alert_level=0
            elif (( temp_int >= 25 && temp_int < 30 )); then
                temp_category="HIGH"
                alert_level=2
            else
                temp_category="CRITICAL"
                alert_level=3
            fi

            # Override alert level if status is already CRITICAL
            if [[ "$status" == "CRITICAL" ]]; then
                alert_level=3
            fi

            echo "${timestamp},${sensor_id},${temperature},${humidity},${status},${temp_category},${alert_level}"
        done
    } > "${TEMP_PROCESSED}.staging"

    # Atomic move
    mv -f "${TEMP_PROCESSED}.staging" "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

    local processed_count
    processed_count=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | wc -l)
    log_info "Processed $processed_count records -> $TEMP_PROCESSED"
    log_transaction "STAGE_2_COMPLETE: $processed_count records processed"

    release_lock
}

# --- Stage 3: Aggregate and generate results ---
stage_aggregate_results() {
    log_info "=== Stage 3: Aggregating results ==="
    log_transaction "STAGE_3_BEGIN"

    if [[ ! -f "$TEMP_PROCESSED" ]]; then
        log_error "Processed file not found: $TEMP_PROCESSED"
        return 1
    fi

    acquire_lock

    # Read from processed temp file and compute aggregates
    local total_records avg_temp avg_humidity critical_count warn_count ok_count
    local max_temp min_temp max_alert

    total_records=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | wc -l)

    # Use awk for aggregation
    read -r avg_temp avg_humidity max_temp min_temp max_alert < <(
        tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | awk -F',' '
        BEGIN { sum_t=0; sum_h=0; max_t=-999; min_t=999; max_a=0; n=0 }
        {
            sum_t += $3; sum_h += $4; n++
            if ($3 > max_t) max_t = $3
            if ($3 < min_t) min_t = $3
            if ($7 > max_a) max_a = $7
        }
        END {
            if (n > 0)
                printf "%.2f %.2f %.1f %.1f %d\n", sum_t/n, sum_h/n, max_t, min_t, max_a
            else
                print "0 0 0 0 0"
        }'
    )

    critical_count=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | awk -F',' '$5 == "CRITICAL"' | wc -l)
    warn_count=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | awk -F',' '$5 == "WARN"' | wc -l)
    ok_count=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | awk -F',' '$5 == "OK"' | wc -l)

    # Build results report
    local results=""
    results+="========================================\n"
    results+="  DATA PROCESSING PIPELINE RESULTS\n"
    results+="  PID: $$\n"
    results+="  Timestamp: $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')\n"
    results+="========================================\n"
    results+="\n"
    results+="SUMMARY STATISTICS:\n"
    results+="  Total Records:      $total_records\n"
    results+="  Avg Temperature:    ${avg_temp}°C\n"
    results+="  Avg Humidity:       ${avg_humidity}%\n"
    results+="  Max Temperature:    ${max_temp}°C\n"
    results+="  Min Temperature:    ${min_temp}°C\n"
    results+="  Max Alert Level:    $max_alert\n"
    results+="\n"
    results+="STATUS BREAKDOWN:\n"
    results+="  OK:       $ok_count\n"
    results+="  WARN:     $warn_count\n"
    results+="  CRITICAL: $critical_count\n"
    results+="\n"
    results+="CRITICAL ALERTS:\n"

    # List critical entries
    local critical_entries
    critical_entries=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | awk -F',' '$7 >= 3 {printf "  [ALERT] Sensor %s: temp=%s°C, humidity=%s%%, status=%s\n", $2, $3, $4, $5}')

    if [[ -n "$critical_entries" ]]; then
        results+="$critical_entries\n"
    else
        results+="  None\n"
    fi

    results+="\n"
    results+="SENSOR DETAILS (from processed data):\n"

    # Re-read the processed temp file for detailed output
    local sensor_details
    sensor_details=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | awk -F',' '{
        printf "  %-6s | Temp: %6s°C | Humidity: %5s%% | Status: %-8s | Category: %-8s | Alert: %s\n",
            $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7
    }')
    results+="$sensor_details\n"

    results+="\n========================================\n"
    results+="  Pipeline completed successfully.\n"
    results+="========================================\n"

    # Atomic write results
    atomic_write "$TEMP_RESULTS" "$(echo -e "$results")"

    log_info "Results written to $TEMP_RESULTS"
    log_transaction "STAGE_3_COMPLETE: results generated"

    release_lock
}

# --- Stage 4: Read and display results ---
stage_display_results() {
    log_info "=== Stage 4: Displaying results ==="
    log_transaction "STAGE_4_BEGIN"

    if [[ ! -f "$TEMP_RESULTS" ]]; then
        log_error "Results file not found: $TEMP_RESULTS"
        return 1
    fi

    # Read from the results temp file
    echo ""
    cat "$TEMP_RESULTS"
    echo ""

    log_transaction "STAGE_4_COMPLETE: results displayed"
}

# --- Stage 5: Read-Modify-Write demonstration ---
stage_update_counters() {
    log_info "=== Stage 5: Atomic read-modify-write (counter update) ==="
    log_transaction "STAGE_5_BEGIN"

    # Initialize a counter file using the same temp path pattern
    local counter_file="/tmp/myapp-$$-counter.tmp"
    TEMP_FILES+=("$counter_file")

    atomic_write "$counter_file" "pipeline_runs=0
records_processed=0
alerts_generated=0"

    # Simulate multiple atomic updates (as if from concurrent operations)
    local total_records
    total_records=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
    local alert_count
    alert_count=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" 2>/dev/null | awk -F',' '$7 >= 2' | wc -l)

    # Atomic read-modify-write: increment pipeline_runs
    atomic_read_modify_write "$counter_file" "sed 's/pipeline_runs=\([0-9]*\)/pipeline_runs=\$((\1 + 1))/; s/\\\$((\\([^)]*\\))/'\"'\"'/' | while IFS= read -r line; do
        if [[ \"\$line\" =~ pipeline_runs= ]]; then
            current=\$(echo \"\$line\" | grep -o '[0-9]*')
            echo \"pipeline_runs=\$((current + 1))\"
        else
            echo \"\$line\"
        fi
    done"

    # Simpler approach: direct locked read-modify-write
    acquire_lock
    {
        # Read current values
        local current_runs current_records current_alerts
        current_runs=$(grep 'pipeline_runs=' "$counter_file" | cut -d= -f2)
        current_records=$(grep 'records_processed=' "$counter_file" | cut -d= -f2)
        current_alerts=$(grep 'alerts_generated=' "$counter_file" | cut -d= -f2)

        # Modify
        current_runs=$((current_runs + 1))
        current_records=$((current_records + total_records))
        current_alerts=$((current_alerts + alert_count))

        # Write atomically
        atomic_write "$counter_file" "pipeline_runs=${current_runs}
records_processed=${current_records}
alerts_generated=${current_alerts}"
    }
    release_lock

    log_info "Updated counters:"
    cat "$counter_file" | while IFS= read -r line; do
        log_info "  $line"
    done

    log_transaction "STAGE_5_COMPLETE: counters updated"
}

# --- Verify temp file integrity ---
verify_temp_files() {
    log_info "=== Verifying temp file integrity ==="

    for f in "$TEMP_INPUT" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" "$TEMP_RESULTS"; do
        if [[ -f "$f" ]]; then
            local size
            size=$(stat -c%s "$f" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
            local lines
            lines=$(wc -l < "$f")
            log_info "  $f: ${size} bytes, ${lines} lines [OK]"
        else
            log_error "  $f: MISSING"
        fi
    done
}

# --- Display transaction log ---
display_transaction_log() {
    log_info "=== Transaction Log ==="
    if [[ -f "$TRANSACTION_LOG" ]]; then
        while IFS= read -r line; do
            echo "  $line"
        done < "$TRANSACTION_LOG"
    fi
}

# ============================================================
# MAIN PIPELINE EXECUTION
# ============================================================

main() {
    log_info "Starting data processing pipeline (PID: $$)"
    log_info "Temp file prefix: /tmp/myapp-$$"
    log_transaction "PIPELINE_START"

    # Initialize transaction log
    echo "" > "$TRANSACTION_LOG"
    log_transaction "PIPELINE_INITIALIZED"

    # Execute pipeline stages
    stage_generate_data
    echo ""

    stage_process_data
    echo ""

    stage_aggregate_results
    echo ""

    stage_display_results
    echo ""

    stage_update_counters
    echo ""

    verify_temp_files
    echo ""

    display_transaction_log

    log_transaction "PIPELINE_COMPLETE"
    log_info ""
    log_info "Pipeline completed successfully!"
    log_info "Temp files will be cleaned up on exit."
}

# Run the pipeline
main "$@"
